UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile, financial statistics, and referral information within the GameFlashX platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, linking to an external authentication system.

totalEarnings.description: The total amount of earnings a user has accumulated and successfully moved from pending to completed status.

pendingEarnings.description: The total amount of earnings a user has accumulated but which are awaiting verification or manual approval.

totalReferrals.description: The total count of users successfully referred by this user.

referralCode.description: A unique code assigned to the user for referral tracking.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

lastActivityAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last activity on the platform.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a category for grouping gift cards (e.g., Shopping, Gaming, Entertainment).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

description.description: A brief description of the category.

GiftCard.title: GiftCard
GiftCard.description: Details about a digital gift card available for users to unlock as a reward.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GiftCard entity.

name.description: The common name of the gift card (e.g., 'Amazon Gift Card').

brand.description: The brand associated with the gift card (e.g., 'Amazon').

description.description: A marketing description of the gift card, potentially AI-generated.

imageUri.description: URL to the image representation of the gift card.

categoryIds.description: References to Categories this gift card belongs to. (Relationship: Category N:N GiftCard)

availableValues.description: An array of numerical values (e.g., 10, 25, 50) for which this gift card is available.

isActive.description: Indicates if the gift card is currently active and available on the platform.

targetMarkets.description: An array of target market codes (e.g., 'US', 'UK', 'CA', 'AU') where this gift card is available.

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a specific task or offer that users can complete to earn rewards.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Task entity.

title.description: The title or name of the task.

description.description: A detailed description of what the task entails.

rewardAmount.description: The monetary reward amount a user receives upon completing this task.

externalUrl.description: The URL where the user needs to go to complete the task.

availableRegions.description: An array of region codes (e.g., 'US', 'UK') where this task is available.

maxCompletionsPerUser.description: The maximum number of times a single user can complete this specific task. Set to 0 for unlimited.

dailyLimitPerUser.description: The maximum number of times a single user can complete this specific task within a 24-hour period. Set to 0 if no daily limit.

isActive.description: Indicates if the task is currently active and available for users.

UserTaskCompletion.title: UserTaskCompletion
UserTaskCompletion.description: Records a specific instance of a user attempting or completing a task, used for tracking and anti-fraud.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserTaskCompletion entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who completed the task. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserTaskCompletion)

taskId.description: Reference to the Task that was completed. (Relationship: Task 1:N UserTaskCompletion)

completionDate.description: Timestamp when the task was reported as completed by the user.

status.description: The current status of the task completion (e.g., awaiting manual review, approved, or rejected).

rewardAmount.description: The reward amount recorded for this specific completion, matching the task's reward at the time of completion.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records all financial movements related to a user's earnings, referrals, and withdrawals.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile associated with this transaction. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Transaction)

amount.description: The monetary amount of the transaction. Positive for earnings, negative for withdrawals.

status.description: The current status of the transaction.

transactionDate.description: Timestamp when the transaction was initiated or occurred.

sourceEntityId.description: Optional reference to the originating entity (e.g., UserTaskCompletion ID for TaskReward, referred UserProfile ID for ReferralBonus).

description.description: A brief description of the transaction for clarity.

RewardUnlock.title: RewardUnlock
RewardUnlock.description: Records an instance where a user successfully 'unlocks' and redeems a digital gift card.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RewardUnlock entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who unlocked the reward. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N RewardUnlock)

giftCardId.description: Reference to the GiftCard that was unlocked. (Relationship: GiftCard 1:N RewardUnlock)

valueUnlocked.description: The specific value of the gift card unlocked (e.g., 25 for a $25 Amazon card).

unlockDate.description: Timestamp when the reward was successfully unlocked.

userLocation.description: The reported location of the user at the time of unlock (e.g., 'UK'), used for live activity feed.

status.description: The status of the reward unlock process.

redeemUrl.description: The external URL provided to the user to redeem the gift card.

definition.description: Stores user-specific profile, financial statistics (totalEarnings, pendingEarnings, totalReferrals), and referral information. Access is strictly limited to the owning user or administrators.
Stores global, publicly viewable categories for grouping gift cards. Read-only for users, writable by administrators.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the category.

definition.description: Stores global, publicly viewable details of gift cards available on the platform. Read-only for users, writable by administrators.

params.name: giftCardId
params.description: The unique identifier of the gift card.

definition.description: Stores global, publicly viewable details of tasks and offers available to users. Read-only for users, writable by administrators.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier of the task.

definition.description: Records individual task completion instances for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning user or administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who completed the task, matching the parent user document.

params.name: completionId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific task completion record.

definition.description: Records all financial transactions (earnings, referrals, withdrawals) for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning user or administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user associated with this transaction, matching the parent user document.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific transaction record.

definition.description: Records individual reward unlock instances for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning user or administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who unlocked the reward, matching the parent user document.

params.name: unlockId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific reward unlock record.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for public display of recent reward unlocks for the 'Live Activity Notifications' feature. Each document is a subset of the 'RewardUnlock' schema, containing only public fields (e.g., valueUnlocked, userLocation, giftCardBrand) populated by a Cloud Function, ensuring privacy and QAPs adherence. Read-only for all users.

params.name: activityId
params.description: The unique identifier for this public activity feed entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The GameFlashX Dashboard Firestore structure prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability following the specified core design principles and mandates. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
*   User-specific data such as `UserTaskCompletion`, `Transaction`, and `RewardUnlock` are stored in subcollections under `/users/{userId}/`. This path-based ownership, where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`, is the primary mechanism for authorization. This design choice *eliminates* the need for `get()` calls to parent documents in security rules to determine access, thereby ensuring Authorization Independence. The `userId` field is also explicitly included within these documents for data integrity and redundancy, enabling atomic creation of parent and child documents. 

**Structural Segregation for Homogeneous Security Posture:**
*   Publicly accessible, static data entities (`Category`, `GiftCard`, `Task`) are placed in top-level collections (`/categories`, `/giftCards`, `/tasks`). These collections are designed for public read access (and admin write), ensuring all documents within them share the same security requirements. 
*   Private user-owned data is strictly confined to `/users/{userId}/` subcollections. 
*   To support the 'Live Activity Notifications' feature, a dedicated top-level collection `/public_reward_activity/{activityId}` is introduced. This collection exclusively contains publicly displayable information (a subset of `RewardUnlock` fields, like `valueUnlocked`, `userLocation`, `giftCardBrand` etc.) populated by a Cloud Function. This segregation prevents exposing sensitive user data from the main `RewardUnlock` records (which are private to the user) and adheres to the QAPs principle by providing a truly public, secure list. A Cloud Function would be responsible for populating this collection upon a user's `RewardUnlock` creation.

**Access Modeling and DBAC:**
*   **Private Data:** User profiles, task completions, transactions, and individual reward unlocks are secured using path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`), where `request.auth.uid` must match the `{userId}` wildcard. This is the most robust form of private data access.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** To implement admin roles without custom claims and while respecting the strict schema reference output constraint, an `isAdmin` boolean field is conceptually added to the `UserProfile` entity (even though it's not present in the provided JSON schema). An admin check in security rules would involve a `get()` call to the authenticated user's own profile (`get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.isAdmin == true`). This method is considered an acceptable deviation from avoiding `get()` calls, as it's a direct self-referential check for a global role, not a hierarchical dependency.

**QAPs (Queries are not Filters):**
*   All collections support secure `list` operations. Public collections (`/categories`, `/giftCards`, `/tasks`, `/public_reward_activity`) allow unfiltered `list` queries as all their documents are intended to be public. 
*   User-owned subcollections enforce QAPs by including the `userId` in the path. Security rules ensure that a user can only `list` documents within *their own* `/users/{userId}/` segment, effectively filtering results by ownership at the security layer rather than relying on client-side queries for filtering.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:**
*   The structure uses clear, semantic names for collections and wildcards (e.g., `users`, `userId`, `taskCompletions`).
*   `status` fields are consistently used for explicit state modeling across `UserTaskCompletion`, `Transaction`, and `RewardUnlock` entities.
